name: Ormco — Spark Aligner (Orthodontic Specialists)
contact_strategy: Prefer the lead orthodontist or practice owner when associated with Damon system content, digital workflow pages, or CBCT technology — a named provider tied to the tech investment is the fastest path to a yes. If no such association is apparent, prefer the practice manager or treatment coordinator; they own scheduling and day-to-day workflow friction. Fall back to the first listed orthodontist. Role names in the brief should reference how Spark reduces their workflow friction, not product features.
description: Independent orthodontic specialists currently using the Damon bracket system or operating an open-ecosystem digital workflow. The ideal target is a Damon-trained orthodontist with non-iTero digital capture (3Shape, Medit, or similar) and in-house CBCT — clinically sophisticated, digitally committed, and not locked to Align Technology's ecosystem. Displacement candidates (Invisalign-exclusive practices) are a secondary call tier: they can A/B test Spark without renegotiating their bracket workflow.
scoring_notes: max_positive = 90 (damon 40 + open_scanner 25 + cbct 25). bullseye_min_score = 85 (lowered from 90 on 2026-06-17 to restore headroom after the Invisalign presence penalty was retired). S-OS-002 (Invisalign tier capture) is now weight 0 — presence is near-universal; contextual only. S-OS-019 (Invisalign Platinum-or-higher) is friction (-15) and caps at Contender — signals deep Align lock-in. Damon listing is required_for_bullseye. Invisalign-exclusive (S-OS-003) is both capped and floored at Contender, pinning displacement candidates exactly in the call queue. MARPE and airway signals carry floor_tier: Contender — niche-fit practices never lost to Manual Review on low score. DSO-locked and GP/pedo-only are hard exclusions. S-OS-020 (Invisalign Top 1%) is a hard exclusion — these practices are not viable displacement targets.

hypothesis.ideal_practice_profile: Single-location or small-group orthodontic specialist, physician-owned, currently using Damon brackets, with an open intraoral scanner (3Shape Trios, Medit, or similar) and on-site CBCT. Not Invisalign-exclusive. Not affiliated with a DSO with a competing aligner contract.
hypothesis.commercial_fit_reasoning: Damon practices already operate within Ormco's bracket system. Spark adds aligner continuity without disrupting the workflow or requiring a second brand relationship. Open digital capture (non-iTero) means the practice is not locked to Align Technology's ecosystem — they can scan to any aligner workflow. CBCT presence signals complex case investment and the precision-case profile where Spark's premium tiers earn the most margin.
hypothesis.fast_close_indicators: Damon listed + open scanner + CBCT = maximum fit score (90/90, bullseye_score ~96). Rep can lead with the bracket-to-aligner continuity story. Practices with two of three pillars score ~67 fit, bullseye_score ~76 — Contender with a direct path to Bullseye on verification.
hypothesis.common_objections: Invisalign contract ownership (counter: Spark's per-case fee structure allows A/B testing without renegotiating); switching costs from iTero workflow (counter: open scanner already in use, no new digital capture investment required); satisfaction with current aligner brand (counter: Damon outcomes already proven — Spark is continuity, not replacement).

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly name the Damon System (Damon Clear, Damon Q, Damon Ultima, or Damon Brackets) as a bracket system or treatment option they offer? Do NOT mark 'yes' based on the orthodontist's name alone — only mark 'yes' if the Damon brand is explicitly listed as a product or treatment.
signals.note: Primary competitive-fit gate. A Damon practice already works within Ormco's system; Spark adds aligner continuity without disrupting the bracket workflow. Cross-sell angle: 'You're already seeing Damon outcomes — Spark gives you bracket-to-aligner continuity without changing your workflow or stocking a second brand.' Confirmed 'no' caps at Contender; not_found caps at Needs Verification.

signals.prompt_instruction: Determine whether this practice offers Invisalign, and if so identify the named provider tier (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Gold Plus, Platinum, Platinum Plus/Elite, Diamond, Diamond Plus). Read tier from page text AND from image alt text, title attributes, and image filenames (e.g. alt='Diamond Provider', 'platinum-badge.png'). Record the specific tier in your reasoning even when it is below Platinum. Mark 'yes' if Invisalign is offered at any tier.
signals.note: CONTEXTUAL / REP-PREP. Invisalign presence is near-universal among orthodontists, so presence is not scored. Captures the specific tier for the rep brief and feeds friction signal S-OS-019.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list Invisalign as their aligner offering AND show no competing aligner brands (no ClearCorrect, Spark, SureSmile, or other non-Invisalign aligners listed)? Mark 'yes' only when both conditions are true: Invisalign is offered AND no other aligner brand appears.
signals.note: FRICTION + CAP + FLOOR. Invisalign-exclusive practices are displacement candidates, not Bullseye. floor_tier: Contender ensures they reach the call queue (bypasses evidence gate on negative-scoring signals); cap_tier: Contender ensures they never exceed Contender. Together they pin the displacement path exactly at Contender. Displacement pitch: 'Your Invisalign contract doesn't own your aligner chair. Spark's per-case fee structure lets you A/B test without renegotiating.' If they also list Damon (S-OS-001=yes), the cap still applies — they become a Contender with strong cross-sell potential, not an acquisition Bullseye.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly identify itself as a Spark aligner provider, or list Spark by name as an offered treatment?
signals.note: ROUTING FLAG ONLY — no score or tier effect. A 'yes' means this is an existing Ormco/Spark account; route to Existing Account / Retention queue, out of the acquisition call queue. Do NOT use exclude_if_yes — the score must not be capped. This flag is for operator-level routing only.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list a competing fixed bracket system — such as 3M Clarity, Carriere, Speed, In-Ovation, or another named bracket brand that is NOT the Damon System — as a treatment they offer?
signals.note: FRICTION (minor). Competing bracket presence adds friction but is not disqualifying. Rep should note the brand for displacement context.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list any clear aligner brand other than Invisalign or Spark — such as ClearCorrect, SureSmile, 3M Clarity Aligners, Reveal, or similar — as an offered treatment?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Multi-brand aligner presence signals an open aligner stack — the practice is not locked to a single system. Rep briefing note only; not scored.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website name the iTero intraoral scanner as equipment they use or offer?
signals.note: FRICTION. iTero is Align Technology's proprietary scanner — an iTero-only practice has its digital capture workflow tightly bound to Invisalign. If the practice also lists a non-iTero scanner (S-OS-008=yes), this friction is suppressed via inhibited_by: a multi-scanner practice is not locked. Friction applies only when the practice is iTero-exclusive.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly name an intraoral scanner other than iTero — such as 3Shape Trios, Medit, Planmeca, Carestream, Dentsply Sirona, or any other non-iTero digital impression system — as equipment they use?
signals.note: Digital sophistication signal, open-ecosystem only. A non-iTero scanner exports open STL files, meaning the practice is not locked to Align's capture workflow. Also suppresses iTero friction (S-OS-007) via inhibited_by when both are present.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly mention CBCT, cone beam CT, or 3D dental imaging as in-office equipment or a service they provide?
signals.note: Clinical sophistication signal. In-house CBCT indicates investment in complex case management and 3D digital workflow — the case types where Spark's precision series adds the most value.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly describe a digital orthodontic workflow, digital treatment planning, or digital case setup — beyond simply offering an intraoral scanner?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Explicit digital workflow language signals clinical readiness for Spark's digital integration. Weight zero — sophistication is captured through the scanner and CBCT signals. Rep briefing note.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website mention any intraoral scanner or digital impression system as equipment they use? This includes any brand: iTero, 3Shape, Medit, Planmeca, Carestream, or other.
signals.note: Broad capture. Specific brand is resolved by S-OS-007 (iTero friction) and S-OS-008 (open scanner benefit). This signal confirms scanner presence when the brand is unclear or unlisted.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly mention indirect bonding as a technique or service they use for bracket placement?
signals.note: Contextual sophistication signal. Indirect bonding indicates case-volume efficiency investment and familiarity with precision lab workflows. Weight zero — informs rep briefing.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly mention MARPE (miniscrew-assisted rapid palatal expansion), micro-implant anchored expansion, or skeletal palatal expansion as a treatment they offer?
signals.note: NICHE QUALIFIER. MARPE cases require high precision and represent the complex-case load where Spark's system earns its margin. Weight zero — niche merit alone does not score, but floor_tier: Contender ensures a MARPE practice is never buried in Manual Review. Niche angle: 'MARPE cases are your hardest bracket workflow — Spark's precision series is built for that load.'

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly describe airway orthodontics, airway-focused treatment, sleep-disordered breathing treatment, or mandibular advancement as a specialty or service area?
signals.note: NICHE QUALIFIER. Airway-focused orthodontics signals advanced clinical positioning and multi-disciplinary case management. floor_tier: Contender ensures these practices reach the call queue. Niche angle: 'Airway-focused ortho is exactly the case type where digital-first brackets pay off fastest.'

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly list complex orthodontic case types such as surgical orthodontics, Class II or Class III correction, extraction cases, TAD (temporary anchorage device) use, or interdisciplinary cases?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Complex case depth maps to Spark's premium product lines and indicates a high-value clinical relationship. Rep briefing note.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website mention a continuing education program, CE courses for referring dentists, study club hosting, or an orthodontic residency training affiliation run by this practice?
signals.note: Rep-prep display signal. A CE-running practice is a clinical influencer — warm-up angle should acknowledge their teaching role. Array contract field decomposed to binary presence. Not scored.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice appear to be part of a DSO (Dental Service Organization) or corporate dental group that has an exclusive aligner contract with a competitor — such as a DSO that is confirmed Invisalign-only or SureSmile-only across its network?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. DSO locked to a rival aligner system is commercially unreachable — procurement sits at corporate, volume commitments are pre-committed. Score capped at 40 by exclusion engine; route to disqualification log. Note: independent practices affiliated with DSOs but with local purchasing authority are NOT this gate.

signals.signal_label: GP or pediatric only (no orthodontic services)
signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice appear to provide ONLY general dentistry or pediatric dentistry with no orthodontic specialty services — no mention of braces, clear aligners, an orthodontist on staff, or orthodontic credentials of any kind?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION (orthodontist_only gate, default: true). Ormco Spark targets specialist orthodontists. A confirmed GP or pedo-only practice is out of scope for acquisition. Score capped at 40 by exclusion engine.

signals.prompt_instruction: Mark 'yes' ONLY if the stated Invisalign tier is Platinum, Platinum Plus, Platinum Elite, Diamond, or Diamond Plus. Do NOT include 'Top 1%' here — that tier is captured by S-OS-020. Detect from page text AND image alt/title attributes and image filenames. Gold, Gold Plus, Silver, Bronze are 'no'. If no tier is stated anywhere, mark 'not_found'.
signals.note: FRICTION + CAP. Platinum through Diamond Plus signals deep Align lock-in. -15 drops a max-sophistication practice (90) into the displacement lane (75). cap_tier Contender permanently bars a high-tier Invisalign practice from acquisition Bullseye. Top 1% providers are separately captured by S-OS-020 (hard exclusion). Confirm against the Invisalign provider locator at QC when the only site evidence is a badge image.

signals.prompt_instruction: Mark 'yes' if this practice is designated as an Invisalign 'Top 1%' provider — the highest Invisalign elite tier. Look for explicit 'Top 1%' text on the page, badge images with 'top-1', 'top1', or 'top 1' in alt text, title attributes, or image filenames, and any language describing an elite or highest-tier Invisalign status. Mark 'not_found' if no tier is stated or if the highest tier mentioned is Diamond Plus or below.
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. Invisalign Top 1% is Align Technology's highest commercial tier — maximum submission volume, maximum rebates, maximum ecosystem dependency. These practices have made a substantial financial and workflow commitment to Align; commercial displacement is not viable in a near-term sales cycle. Route to long-term tracking only.
